text:
Machi Komacine (マチ＝コマチネ, Machi Komachine) is member #3 of the Phantom Troupe. Her physical strength ranked sixth among the group. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 84 

Appearance

Machi is a short, young woman with a petite build. She has blue eyes and pink, messy hair that reaches the middle of her back, but she most often wears it tied up. She is one of the few Troupe members that prefers to wear traditional Japanese-style clothing, looking like a modern-day kunoichi (female ninja). Her usual outfit consists of short tights matched with a short-sleeved uwagi, an obi sash worn around her waist and an obijime tied over it, a pair of fingerless gloves with a pincushion, tabi boots or socks with zōri, and warmers that cover her lower legs.

Her spider tattoo has the number 3 on the spider's abdomen, but the tattoo location has yet to be revealed.

In the 1999 anime adaptation, her eyes are brown and her hair is blue. Her hair is changed to dark purple during the OVAs.

Personality

Machi is one of the coldest members of Troupe, almost never losing her cool or displaying any emotions. However, according to Shalnark, she is a "nice person" at heart. She has been shown to care for Pakunoda, as she was one of the members who mourned her death and watched over her grave. Her caring side was also shown when she was much younger as when Sarasa was brutally murdered; Machi hugged the bag Sarasa was put in tightly and told the group that they needed to take her home. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 397 Furthermore, Machi is fiercely loyal to her fellow Spiders and especially to Chrollo, going so far as to challenge two other Troupe members for his sake. She also stated that she would hunt down Hisoka if he were to kill Chrollo. She is very confident in her strength and replies accordingly if threatened.

Machi also has sharp intuitions, which have turned out to be true most of the time and are trusted by some of her comrades, such as Nobunaga Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 89 and Chrollo. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 108 Hisoka seems to have high regard for Machi and her abilities, although she treats him as a nuisance at best and a potential enemy of Chrollo's at worst. However, she seemed to care for him to some extent after he died, and when he was revived, she merely told him that she hoped he had learned his lesson, although her job was to confirm his death. When Hisoka restrained her, she revealed a much wilder side, screaming in anger at him. 

Machi, growing up in Meteor City, was influenced by the conditions there, sharing remarkable bonds that have been described as "thinner than water, and yet thicker than blood". Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 102 This was also proven by the fact that the Phantom Troupe is a major asset in the defense of the city. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 22, Chapter 224 

Like the other members of the Troupe, Machi is also cold-blooded, ruthless, and not above inhumane mass murder, most notably when she brutally massacred the Kurta Clan alongside her teammates, which involved the torture of innocent people, including children, and the desecration of their bodies. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 0, Chapter 2 The traps she made for the Mafiosi were particularly brutal, with her victims having their necks broken as they were squeezed by the wires. The gruesome murder of their dear friend Sarasa and their thirst for revenge against the perpetrators have desensitized her to such atrocities. Machi does not seem to put too much value on her own life, a trait shared by all of the Spiders. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 104 

Background

Machi originated from Meteor City About fifteen years prior to her appearance in at Heavens Arena, Machi spotted Chrollo, Shalnark, and Franklin running with a videotape and tossing it from hand to hand. She reported the fact to Uvogin, who got in the boys' way. He knocked out Shalnark, and got into a fight with Franklin, while Chrollo got away. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 395 Machi was with Uvogin to witness the film Power Cleaners after Chrollo and his three friends dubbed over it. When Chrollo imitated the villain's character, Machi and the other children were most impressed by the character he played, showing unprecedented emotions. Machi then watched the events unfolding between Uvogin and Chrollo, where the former expected the latter to return the videotape that was taken in Uvo's territory. However, Sarasa came in between the two and 'dubbed' over what Uvogin really wanted to say to Chrollo after the film and live performance, expressing his awe and amazement. The group then smiled in approval, but when everyone chose their roles to help with the next screening, Machi refused and did not want to take part. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 396 

Eventually, she did participate but was offered the role of 'princess' by Uvo, which she strongly said no to. Machi exclaimed that she would rather be a 'bad guy' than be a 'princess.' When Sarasa disappeared, a search ensued, and all of the children (including the Troupe members) who attended the screening were looking for her at Chrollo's request. Machi, alongside Pakunoda, Phinks, and Feitan, stood on massive heaps of garbage to call out and look for Sarasa. As Chrollo and Uvogin looked to the Forest, they found a bag hanging on a tree that was revealed to be Sarasa's mangled body.

After Uvo and Chrollo argued about what her murderers wrote, Machi shouted at Uvo, who turned to leave, asking if he would leave Sarasa's body behind. She then hugged the bag containing Sarasa's body tightly, requesting that she be taken home and sympathizing with the pain Sarasa went through. Paku and Sheila cried in response, and then Machi apologized sincerely to Sarasa's lifeless body. After the funeral, she joined Uvogin and six others in pledging their lifelong loyalty to Chrollo. Then she took some time to train using Nen under Renko in Meteor City's Kirimori Valley. 

Three years later, Machi was present during the foundation of Phantom Troupe, along with Franklin, Feitan, Pakunoda, Nobunaga, Uvogin, Shalnark and Chrollo. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 102 

As a founding member of the troupe, she took part in the massacre of the Kurta Clan. 

Plot

Heavens Arena arc

Machi is hired by Hisoka to patch him up after his match against Kastro at Heavens Arena. She watches the match and waits for Hisoka in the tunnel nearby. She congratulates him on his victory, but then calls him an idiot for willingly taking that much damage. She uses her Nen ability to reattach his severed arms, charging him 70 million in total. Machi mentally commends Hisoka for his speed in hiding his new sutures with his Texture Surprise, then tells Hisoka that the Phantom Troupe gathering in Yorknew City scheduled for the end of August has become compulsory, and warns him that Chrollo might personally punish him if he skips another job. Hisoka decides to ask her if she is free for dinner, but turns as he asks and sees that she had already left. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 7, Chapter 55 

Yorknew City arc

On August 31 st , Machi is seen with Franklin, Nobunaga, and Feitan as they walk through the Gordeau Desert to Yorknew City. Later, when the Troupe convene in an abandoned building, Chrollo announces to Machi and the other members that they would steal all the items at the Underground Auction. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 71 On the evening of September 1 st , Machi and six other Spiders infiltrate the Cemetery Building and kill all Mafia members and clients present, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 73 but discover that the items had already been removed from the vault. They report the matter to Chrollo as they escape in a hot-air balloon to the Gordeau Desert whilst chased being by dozens of mafia members seeking revenge. From their report, Chrollo speculates that a Shadow Beast named Owl had been tipped off and secured the items before they could be stolen. Chrollo instructs them to create a big enough scene using the chasing mafia members to lure out the Shadow Beasts. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 74 

The Spiders land their hot-air balloon in the desert, and Uvogin engages the chasing mafia members by himself as the others wait. Machi, Shalnark, and Shizuku play cards as Uvogin massacres their Mafia pursuers Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 75 and four Shadow Beasts by himself. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 76 Uvogin is caught off guard and captured in a moment of relaxation by Kurapika, but Machi immediately uses a needle and a Nen thread to track Uvogin and she and four other Spiders pursue Uvogin by car. Right as they catch up, Kurapika notices the thread and Machi's needle is removed, but right before the Spiders catch up with Kurapika, the remaining Shadow Beasts arrive and engage them. The Spiders stop to engage the Shadow Beasts and learn the identity of Uvogin's abductor, and the remaining Shadow Beasts except for Owl are killed, who is captured and tortured for the location of the auction items. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 77 

Near midnight, they find where Uvogin is being held captive and free him after Phinks kills Dalzollene. In the early hours of September 2 nd , Machi tells Uvogin about the items and the deaths of the Shadow Beasts, but he insists on finding and killing the chain user instead of returning to the Troupe's hideout with them. She and the other members have no choice but to let Uvogin do what he wants. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 79 Soon afterward, Uvogin is killed by Kurapika in a duel in the Gordeau Desert, and Chrollo tells the Troupe members that they will change their plans if Uvogin doesn’t return by dawn. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 84 

On September 3 rd , Machi and Nobunaga wait in the middle of a square in an attempt to lure out the chain user, but they attract Gon and Killua's attention instead and are tailed by them. With the help of Phinks and Pakunoda, who have been secretly following them, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 90 they easily capture the two boys and bring them back to their hideout. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 91 

In the evening, she and the other members excluding Nobunaga Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 92 launch a retaliatory attack on the Cemetery Building, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 96 slaughtering police and 2,000 Mafia members. Machi, despite pairing up with Hisoka, goes into the battlefield by herself, killing several mafia members to to manipulate their guns with her threads, shooting down everyone who approaches her. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 98 After the death of the Ten Dons, the Troupe members take over the auction and have Kortopi create copies of the items. Backstage, Hisoka distracts Bean while Machi uses her Nen thread to hang and kill him. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 100 Kortopi then creates fake corpses of five of the Troupe members (including Machi) to trick the Mafia into thinking that some of them were killed during the encounter. Afterward, Machi celebrates the triumph with the rest of the Troupe back at their hideout. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 101 

In the afternoon of September 4 th , at the Troupe's hideout, Nobunaga insists that they keep pursuing the chain user instead of leaving Yorknew. Knowing that it’s impossible to dissuade him, Chrollo uses Neon's stolen ability to write out fortunes for Nobunaga and the other members, eventually learning that five more of them will die if they meet the chain user within two weeks. They then intend to return to Meteor City to avoid that misfortune, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 104 but Hisoka alters his own fortune with Texture Surprise and convinces the whole group to stay in Yorknew to take care of the chain user. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 106 

Later, Chrollo is able to deduce that the chain user is connected to Neon and is the last member of the Kurta Clan who wants to obtain the Scarlet Eyes. After Kortopi tracks the copies that were sold at the auction, Chrollo uses a map to work out that the chain user is at the Hotel Beitacle. He then orders Kortopi, Machi, Nobunaga, Pakunoda, and Shizuku to go with him to the hotel. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 109 On the way there, Chrollo notices that they are being followed by two people, telling Kortopi, Nobunaga, and Pakunoda to go ahead while he, Machi, and Shizuku stay behind. The three Spiders prepare for the upcoming battle, but are surprised by Gon and Killua coming out of the shadows, Machi then asks Chrollo if they should kill the children, but Chrollo says he trusts Machi's instincts, and that it would be better for them to stay alive if they are connected to Kurapika, nonetheless, Chrollo orders Machi to kill them if they try to escape. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 111 

Chrollo, Machi, and Shizuku take Gon and Killua to the hotel Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 112 and are eventually joined by Pakunoda's group in the lobby. Chrollo tells Pakunoda to check the boys’ memories again, but once she does and before she can reveal what she has learned about Kurapika, a power outage suddenly happens, allowing Gon and Killua to try to make an escape. Despite the total darkness, Machi easily recaptures Killua even after he breaks some of her ribs while Nobunaga catches Gon. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 113 However, when the light comes back on, they realize that Chrollo has been captured by the chain user. In Pakunoda's flashback remembering the formation of the Troupe in Meteor City, Machi can be seen among the founding members listening to Chrollo's speech about the importance of the Spider. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 114 

Phinks, Feitan, and Shalnark soon arrive at the hotel. The Troupe then receives a call from Kurapika, who tells them not to follow him, not to hurt the hostages, and to give the phone to Pakunoda. He then tells Pakunoda to go to Lingon Airport alone to negotiate the terms of a hostage exchange and that the other members have to return to their hideout. Phinks, Feitan, and Shalnark try to follow Pakunoda, but Nobunaga stops them after he fears that Chrollo will be killed, leading to a heated quarrel between Nobunaga and Phinks. Because Machi and Shizuku agree with Nobunaga, and Kurapika tells them about Melody's ability to detect lies, they all return to the hideout as ordered. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 115 

Pakunoda returns from the airport and tells the others that she is leaving with just the two boys. Phinks becomes so angry that he wants to get rid of the boys and then go after the chain user, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 117 but Machi disagrees with him. She and Kortopi side with Pakunoda against Phinks and Feitan, who think that she and the others are being manipulated by the chain user. After their quarrel is interrupted by Gon, and Franklin stresses that any more in-fighting will be the death of the Spider, Phinks finally agrees to let Pakunoda leave and to see what happens. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 118 

After successfully carrying out the hostage exchange, Pakunoda returns without Chrollo and fires six of her Memory Bombs into Machi and five other members, quickly collapsing afterward. Shizuku checks her body and confirms that she's dead, asking the others what happened. Phinks then explains everything to the others. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 119 

Greed Island arc

On September 6 th , Phinks and Feitan steal a copy of Greed Island from Battera and bring it back to the hideout. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 122 Phinks invites Machi and the others to play, but they decline or offer to play later. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 123 On September 10 th , Machi is seen talking with Bonolenov near Pakunoda's grave as Shalnark convinces Shizuku and Kortopi to enter Greed Island. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 14, Chapter 128 After Shalnark discovers that Greed Island is an island in the real world and that there's an exorcist on the island, Machi enters the game with the other members to search for them. They are forced to cooperate with Hisoka because Chrollo asked him personally to find the exorcist who will remove Kurapika’s Judgment Chain from around his heart. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 149 

After meeting up with the others, Hisoka is told by Shalnark that Machi and Nobunaga are tailing the exorcist. Shalnark shows Machi’s thread to Hisoka and tells him to follow it. Phinks stresses the importance of the job, and Hisoka knows that it’s his only chance to fight Chrollo. Hisoka meets up with Machi and Nobunaga to spy on the disguised exorcist. He then senses Kalluto skulking in the shadows and realizes that it was his ability that allowed the Troupe to find the exorcist. After they introduce Kalluto as Hisoka’s replacement and Hisoka seems interested in him, Machi and Nobunaga warn Kalluto to be careful since Hisoka will go after anything. Hisoka assures Nobunaga that he will negotiate with the exorcist, and Machi asks him if he actually intends to fight Chrollo. Hisoka replies that he does and asks her who she wants to survive, with Machi quickly telling him that he can drop dead once he finishes the job with the exorcist. Hisoka then asks her what she will do if he ends up killing Chrollo, and Machi sternly tells him that she will chase him to the ends of the earth and kill him. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 17, Chapter 170 

Succession Contest arc

After Hisoka’s deathmatch against Chrollo at Heavens Arena, Machi, Shalnark, and Kortopi stand over Hisoka’s body to confirm that his death. Shalnark and Kortopi are satisfied and leave, but Machi stays behind to clean Hisoka up since he had paid in advance to do so. Kortopi whispers to Shalnark that Machi wouldn't accept money when Hisoka chose to fight Chrollo, and Shalnark whispers back that Machi is a nice person at heart. After the two leave, Machi thanks Hisoka for helping to exorcise Chrollo as she observes the damage to his face and neck. Right as Machi activates her Nen to stitch Hisoka up, aura surrounds Hisoka’s body and leaves Machi stunned before she realizes that it must be post-mortem Nen. Hisoka comes back to life and asks Machi if he had really died. Hisoka comments on the difficulty of fighting someone of Chrollo's caliber under their ideal conditions, and Machi sighs before telling him to pick his battles next time if he had learnt his lesson. She tells him to sit down while she stitches him up, but Hisoka declines and uses his Texture Surprise to cover his wounds and give himself multiple prostheses. After Machi turns and prepares to leave, he restrains her with Bungee Gum and asks her to tell the other Spiders that he will fight any of them on sight until he has killed them all. Enraged, Machi threatens to kill him on the spot and demands that he remove his gum, but Hisoka tells her to get it off by herself, further enraging Machi. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 357 

On the second day of the Black Whale’s voyage, Machi comes across Chrollo at a crowded market in Tier 5 with a sad look on her face. She tells Chrollo that she would kill him, but he does not allow her to do so. In case Chrollo's refusal was because he wanted to kill Hisoka as well, Machi tries saying that she was the first to state her intent to do so, but Chrollo continues to refuse, and refuses her response of deciding by a coinflip. Chrollo reminds her that coins are for disputes between two individuals, but this time, every member of the Troupe wants to kill Hisoka. He tells her to just find Hisoka, guaranteeing that he is somewhere on the ship. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 371 

On the fourth day of the voyage, the Troupe gathers together in the crowded central dining hall to discuss the situation regarding Hisoka. The Cha-R Family eventually approach them and ask them to leave, and Chrollo directs them to do so without resistance. As they leave, Chrollo states that they will meet again at that table and orders them to bring him Hisoka's head, before the Troupe members going their separate ways. Machi is last seen by herself in a standard cabin on Tier 5. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 380 

On the seventh day of the voyage, after Phinks, Nobunaga, and Feitan discuss the current situation with Cha-R superiors Ken'i and Tajao, Phinks asks them to verify Heil-Ly boss Morena's location while they contact the other Troupe members to hunt down the hit man attacking the Cha-R. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 384 

On the tenth day of the voyage, after Xi-Yu boss Onior confirms with his underboss Hinrigh that Hisoka is not on Tier 4, Onior directs Hinrigh to permit the Spiders to search Tier 4. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 390 

On the twelfth day of the voyage, after investigating the Heil-Ly Family Secret Hideout with Hinrigh Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 398 and being forcibly teleported out, Nobunaga reunites with Phinks and Feitan. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 400 Phinks and Feitan decide to head towards the transmitter that Hinrigh hid in the Heil-Ly Family Secret Hideout, but Nobunaga stops them and says that he has something to ask the Cha-R superiors, and that they might need to call on the other Spiders depending on the answer to his question. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 404 The three find the Cha-R superiors and Nobunaga shares his hypothesis that the current Heil-Ly are not like the Heil-Ly that the Cha-R know, acting like the Spiders did by having the individual members decide the best way to fulfil the overall strategy their leader decides. The Cha-R officially ask the Spiders to eliminate the Heil-Ly, and as they head out to do so, Phinks asks if they can help inform the remaining Spiders. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 405 

Equipment

Sewing Needles: Machi keeps several needles in the pincushion she wears on her wrist. She uses them in conjunction with her Nen ability to stitch wounds Hunter × Hunter - Volume 7, Chapter 55 and track targets. 

Abilities & Powers

Due to Machi being born in Meteor City, her existence is not recorded in any database, which makes obtaining information on her nearly impossible. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 105 As a member of the Phantom Troupe and a thief with a Class-A bounty, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 1, Chapter 2 Machi is an exceptional and experienced fighter, being stated to be a professional murderer, with killing being part of her daily life. She is able to overpower competent Nen users such as the Shadow Beasts, and both Gon and Killua in multiple situations. She has extreme confidence in her strength, demonstrated when she was willing to engage in combat with both Phinks and Feitan in order to let Pakunoda go to the hostage exchange with Kurapika. She also confidently told Hisoka that she would kill him if he ever managed to kill Chrollo. Furthermore, when Gon and Leorio couldn't grasp the extent of her and Nobunaga's strength, Killua compared it to that of Hisoka. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 88 When dividing the Troupe into groups to prevent Kurapika from killing them one by one, Chrollo placed Machi in a team with two out of the five members who had received a foreboding fortune and possessed abilities that were said to be invaluable assets to the Spiders. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 108 

Like the rest of the Troupe, Machi has superhuman physical attributes but has yet to fight with her bare hands (although the guard she used to defend against Killua and Hisoka suggests that she has some expertise in the field). She primarily resorts to using her threads in combat, restraining or hanging her opponents, or even setting elaborate traps. She is also rather observant, understanding that professionals were watching her and Nobunaga. Hinrigh also noted his inferiority compared to the Spiders in general, mentioning that he and the Kakin mafia wouldn't stand a chance against them. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 393 

Preternatural Perception: Machi has been shown to possess what some may refer to as a sixth sense, gathering information from her surroundings through no discernible means. She knew that she and Nobunaga were being watched by Gon, Killua, and Leorio (although she could not discern their positions). Perhaps more impressively, she was aware of Gon and Killua tailing her and Nobunaga while the two were in a state of Zetsu, with the latter praising their skill in the technique. Unlike Gon and Killua, she was able to sense that there were other pursuers as well. According to Kurapika, Machi (and the other Spiders) could sense Kurapika's hostile intent before attacking them, even though they were meters away. Killua also mentioned that vigilance is in her blood. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 110 

Enhanced Strength: The fact that she ranked sixth in arm-wrestling within the Phantom Troupe is a testament to her brawn. She can easily lift several adult men, hung by her wires, and managed to immobilize a physically strong individual like Killua by wrapping her arms around him. 

Immense Speed and Reflexes: A testament to Machi's speed is first shown when she flawlessly stitches Hisoka's severed arms in a matter of seconds, with her hands looking like a mere blur. After he unleashed his deafening howl and the other members asked why he didn’t warn them, Uvogin replied that they were fast enough to cover their ears before the sound reached them. She managed to react fast enough after Uvogin was restrained by Kurapika's Chain Jail, throwing a Nen-imbued needle onto his calf to keep track of him. Most remarkable of all, she was able to notice that Gon was going to move, appear behind him, and put her hands on his shoulders before he could even catch a glimpse of her or make a single step. According to Kurapika, an attack that lasted 0.5 seconds would be more than enough time for the Spiders to avoid it. She can run on walls without any difficulty, and was stated to be faster than a car in rush hour, with Kurapika stating that Machi was fast. The second she noticed killing intent coming from Killua, Machi blocked her neck. Machi also quickly blocked her neck when Hisoka attacked her from behind. 

Enhanced Agility: Machi can run on the sides of buildings and easily jump horizontally from one to another. She can also easily climb multiple floors of a building in a matter of seconds. 

Immense Endurance: Machi is extremely resistant to pain. Even though Killua stabbed her in the chest, breaking some of her ribs and ripping through muscle, she still managed to restrain him while keeping Gon bound. Despite the multiple fractures, she was later seen moving, breathing, and speaking normally. 

Muscle Control: Machi has excellent control over her muscles, preventing Killua's Nen-enhanced hand from reaching her vitals and then keeping it in place. She seems to be able to close her injuries by contracting her muscles, with no blood appearing on her clothes even though her chest was pierced. 

Keen Intellect: Machi managed to figure out the strategy that Hisoka used to defeat Kastro, proving herself to be quite intelligent. She also proved herself to be a quick thinker in battle, instantly throwing a needle to track Uvogin after he was captured by Kurapika. 

Immense Intuition: According to Chrollo and Nobunaga, Machi's hunches are often accurate. Her feeling that Uvogin was dead and that Gon and Killua were connected to the chain user proved to be correct, even when Gon and Killua themselves did not know about Kurapika's involvement with the Troupe. 

Expert in Stealth: Machi can sneak up on multiple enemies and kill them without being noticed. This characteristic is reminiscent of ninjitsu and suits her kunoichi appearance.

Master Thrower: Machi can throw her needles with great accuracy, managing to tag Uvogin while he was being kidnapped by Kurapika. 

Nen

Machi is a Transmuter with dual affinity toward Enhancement Exhibition: Togashi Yoshihiro -Puzzle- and a remarkable talent, having been able to see aura as a kid even before being properly 'awakened', making her a Nen genius. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 6, Chapter 48 She is proficient in Hatsu, In, Ren, and Gyo, with the probable addition of Ken and, obviously, Ten. Outside of her natural category, she appears to have at least a working command of Emission. 

Manga Appearances

Battles

Quotes

(To Hisoka) "I was never quite sure before, but that match proved it to me. You're an idiot." 
(To the other Spiders) "In makes my thread invisible. Unless they use Gyo or notice the needle, this thread will track them to the ends of the earth." 
(To Nobunaga) "What are you talking about? You're the one who's telling me what to do." 
(To Gon) "It’s no use. Don’t try anything funny." 
(To Killua) "The darkness won’t hide all that bloodlust. Besides, you should aim for the neck, though I blocked you anyway." 
(To Hisoka) "You can drop dead when your job is done." 
(To Hisoka) "Thanks for helping to exorcise Chrollo..." 

Trivia

In the official databook, her name is also spelled as "Matiy". The Volume 34 extras confirm this spelling and reveal her surname, "Kohmatiyneit".
Machi's name, if spelled with kanji, could be "襠"—an article of clothing Gusset, a piece of material added to reduce stress in tight-fitting clothes. It could also be "待ち", "waiting"—referring to her patient and calm personality.
Machi is the first Troupe member to be shown, excluding Hisoka, who was just pretending to be one.
Machi is the only woman Hisoka has seemingly shown romantic interest in when he asks her to have dinner with him. 
Machi appears to be the only Troupe member aware of Hisoka's Texture Surprise ability. 
According to the Volume 34 extras, Machi was supposed to be killed by Hisoka at the end of the fight between him and Chrollo, but Togashi eventually dismissed the idea. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chrollo vs. Hisoka: A Commentary 
Machi and Nobunaga appear at a ramen shop in a Volume 32 extra.

Anime and Manga Differences

In the 1999 anime adaptation, Machi witnesses Hisoka fighting Gon in the Heavens Arena. When Gon is captured by her and the other Spiders, she asks Hisoka why he didn't talk to Gon if he already knew him.
In the 2011 anime adaptation, it is visually suggested that Machi also has eidetic (photographic) memory, as shown when she seemingly "takes a picture" of Hisoka's wounds with her own eyes before starting to stitch/heal his arms. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 32 (2011) 

In the 2011 anime adaptation, while Kurapika was talking with Uvogin about the Kurta Clan massacre, Machi's Nen Stitches were seen in the background with dead people hanging from it. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 47 (2011) 

Miscellaneous

In the second character popularity poll, Machi placed 7 th with 544 votes.
In the third character popularity poll, Machi placed 12 th with 282 votes.
Kuon Sayaka plays Machi in the stage play, Hunter × Hunter: Real Stage.
Machi's character song from the 2011 anime adaptation, Threads - cruel spider -, was performed by her Japanese voice actress (Rena Maeda).
